title: Avatar: Fire and Ash Dominates Early 2026 Box Office
text: James Cameron's Avatar: Fire and Ash led the domestic box office during the first weekend of 2026, earning $40 million from 3,825 theaters in its third weekend of release. The film declined 35% from the previous post-Christmas weekend. The sci-fi epic has accumulated $306 million domestically and reached $1.08 billion globally after just 18 days in theaters. However, it crossed the billion-dollar milestone more slowly than its predecessors—taking 18 days compared to 14 days for Avatar: The Way of Water and 17 days for the original Avatar. Disney's Zootopia 2 held steady at number two with $19 million, while the psychological thriller The Housemaid climbed to third place with $15.2 million. Timothée Chalamet's Marty Supreme remained in fourth position with $12.5 million. According to Comscore analyst Paul Dergarabedian, With a killer slate on the docket, there's confidence that 2026 will be the biggest year for theaters since 2020. Early 2026 box office revenues are already pacing 26.5% ahead of 2025's performance.
